schema_note: Flat array of renewal records. One state can have multiple records (different license_type, cohort, or renewal group). generate.py groups records by state_slug to build one page per state. next_deadline_computed is the actual next upcoming deadline computed from as_of_date forward -- it is NOT a static fact, it must be recomputed if this file is regenerated on a later date. null means the record's pattern is not resolvable to a single date from state-level facts alone (birth-month rules with no personalization, or a firm-cycle anchor year not present in verified source data) -- in those cases see computation/cohort_groups/data_gap_note for how the page should handle it instead of asserting a fake date.
spike_source: b3_saas/day0_spike_results.md -- 10-state Day-0/7 spike, 2026-07-03. CPA-only scope; contractor licensing dropped as structurally fragmented (no single state-level license in 6/10 sampled states).

records.cycle_description: Individual CPA license renewal is a rolling 2-year 'reestablishment period' anchored to each CPA's own original certificate date, not a publicly stated odd/even calendar split. Real active licenses show both odd- and even-year expirations simultaneously (confirmed via DBPR's live license-verification tool), so there is no single date DeadlineRadar can safely publish for every individual visitor.
records.data_gap_note: We previously listed this as two odd/even cohort records, but a 2026-07-05 recovery review found that framing was wrong: individual CPA expiration dates are per-licensee, tied to each person's own original certificate date -- not a public odd/even rule. The codified odd-year rule applies to FIRM licenses only (see Florida's firm page), not individuals. Confirm your exact personal expiration date using the Florida DBPR license-verification lookup (myfloridalicense.com) or on your license.

records.cycle_description: Firm licenses expire December 31 of odd-numbered years (a single cohort, simpler than the individual-license split).
records.data_gap_note: We could not confirm the specific 'odd-numbered years' detail against two independent authoritative sources: the Board's own page states firm licenses renew biennially and expire December 31 of odd years, but neither Fla. Stat. 473.3101 nor Florida Administrative Code chapters 61H1-26/61H1-31 state the odd-year parity -- they only confirm a generic 2-year cycle. Confirm your exact renewal date with the Florida Board of Accountancy or on your license.

records.cycle_description: Individual CPA license expires September 30, every 3 years. Current cycle ends 2027-09-30.
Firm licenses expire November 30, every 3 years (68 Ill. Admin. Code 1420.80(b)) -- the same rule section that governs individual renewals, just a different subsection.
records.data_gap_note: The recurring month/day (November 30) and 3-year cycle length are confirmed, but we do not have a specific anchor year for the firm track (unlike the individual track, which has a confirmed current cycle ending 2027-09-30). Confirm your firm's exact renewal date with the Illinois Dept. of Financial and Professional Regulation or on your permit.

records.cycle_description: CPA license expires December 31 of odd-numbered years. Current cycle ends 2027-12-31.
records.data_gap_note: We could not confirm this date against two independent authoritative sources: the Board's own renewal-information page clearly states the cycle (December 31, odd-numbered years), but the only candidate second source we could find -- a 2012 Pennsylvania Bulletin rulemaking about CPE ethics-hour requirements -- only coincidentally references a 2012-2013 compliance window and does not independently state the general biennial/odd-year renewal rule, so it does not count as real corroboration. Confirm your exact renewal date with the Pennsylvania State Board of Accountancy or on your license.

records.cycle_description: Individual CPA license expires December 31 of odd-numbered years. Current cycle ends 2027-12-31.
Firm licenses expire June 30 of even-numbered years. Next: 2028-06-30.

ALL CPA licenses (active, probationary, and CPA-retired) must be renewed, or a change to inactive status requested, by the end of the day on June 30 every year. This applies every year to every licensee — it is not a multi-year cycle. Missing the June 30 cutoff (i.e. failing to act before July 1) triggers a Letter of Demand. Next: 2027-06-30.
records.data_gap_note: We could not confirm a single date against two independent authoritative sources: the Board's current page says the renewal deadline is June 30, but the underlying rule (21 NCAC 08J .0101, readopted effective June 1, 2026) states renewal is due 'by the first day of July' (July 1) -- a one-day conflict between two current official sources. Confirm your exact renewal date with the North Carolina State Board of CPA Examiners or on your license.
records.verification_note: Corrected during sprint-task-5 adversarial verify: initial spike data used 'before July 1' loosely and computed a July 1 deadline; re-checked directly against the NC Board's own 2025-2026 renewal page, which states the cutoff is June 30 (July 1 is when the Letter-of-Demand process starts). Fixed to June 30 with the primary source URL updated accordingly. NOTE (2026-07-05 correctness audit): that source page has since gone dead (404) and the underlying rule itself could not be re-confirmed -- downgraded to a data gap pending re-verification.

records.cycle_description: Individual CPA license expires biennially on July 31. Neither official source specifies which year of the 2-year cycle applies to a given licensee (the earlier 'December 31 of odd years' figure in our dataset traced only to a third-party CPE-vendor blog, not an official source, and has been dropped).
records.data_gap_note: The renewal rule itself is now double-sourced (Michigan LARA's current licensing page plus a separately-hosted official LARA licensing-guide PDF both confirm: biennial, expires July 31), but neither document states which year of the 2-year cycle applies to a given license holder, so we cannot safely publish one 'next July 31' date for every visitor. Confirm your exact renewal date with the Michigan Board of Accountancy (LARA) or on your license.

records.cycle_description: CPA permits expire December 31, with a January 31 grace period, but only about one third of Ohio CPAs are due in any given year due to a rotating 3-group cohort system. Which group a CPA is in determines which December 31 applies to them.
records.grace_period_note: A January 31 grace period follows each December 31 deadline before a permit is considered expired.

records.cycle_description: CPA license expires every 2 years at midnight on the last day of the licensee's own birth month. Licensees with an odd birth year renew in odd expiration years; licensees with an even birth year renew in even expiration years.
Renewal fee is due annually by the last day of the licensee's own birth month.
Mandatory triennial registration (distinct from the license itself) expires in the month prior to the licensee's own birth month, 2-3 years after first registration.

computation.note: Unlike California/Texas, this expiration date depends on the individual's own first-registration date, not birth month alone -- a personal fact we don't have. Confirm your registration renewal date with NYSED or on your registration certificate.

records.cycle_description: Triennial (3-year) renewal cycle. The Board's own FAQ confirms the triennial structure, that renewal notices are mailed about 60 days before the expiration date printed on the license, and that CPE (120 hours per triennial period, minimum 20/year) is tracked over the same period. The Board's page does not itself publish the specific current-cycle end date.
records.data_gap_note: NJ renews on a fixed triennial cycle ending December 31, but the specific current-cycle end date is not published at the state-board level. Confirm your exact expiration on your license certificate or with the New Jersey State Board of Accountancy.

records.cycle_description: NJ registers accounting firms separately from individuals; firm registration is also triennial, but whether the firm cycle is synchronized to the same Jan 1-Dec 31 calendar as individual licenses, or runs on a separate schedule, is not stated on any official New Jersey Board page we could find.
records.data_gap_note: We could not confirm a specific firm renewal date from an official New Jersey source. Confirm your firm's exact renewal date with the New Jersey State Board of Accountancy or on your registration.

records.cycle_description: Annual renewal, fixed calendar date. All Virginia CPAs holding an Active or Inactive individual license must renew by June 30 each year; the renewal window opens March 1. Licenses not renewed automatically go to Expired status on July 1 (reinstatement fee applies). No separate CPE reporting deadline -- CE compliance is self-certified as part of the renewal itself, with supporting documentation retained for 4 years in case of audit.

Annual renewal, same fixed June 30 calendar date as individual licenses; renewal window opens March 1. Unrenewed firm licenses automatically go to Expired status on July 1, after which the firm may not provide attest or compilation services until reinstated ($500 reinstatement fee).

Biennial (every 2 years), expiring June 30. The Commonwealth's official Division of Occupational Licensure fee/renewal-schedule page (licensing.reg.state.ma.us, board_code=PA) confirms 'Biennial (2 years)' and '30-Jun / ODD & EVEN YEAR' for individual CPAs, i.e. renewals are split across odd and even years board-wide. CPE is 80 hours per 2-year period (4 hours ethics, no carryover) per 252 CMR 2.14. Orchestrator independent re-check (2026-07-05) found no support for a license-number-parity assignment rule -- mass.gov states biennial + June 30 for all, with no odd/even-by-license-number mechanic. That claim is REMOVED; which specific year (odd or even) applies to a given licensee is treated as an individual-license unknown, same shape as Washington/New York's gaps.

computation.note: Renewal is biennial, always on June 30, but which of the two years (odd or even) a given licensee is due in is not resolvable from a general rule -- it is specific to that licensee's own license record. Do not ask users for their license-number parity; check with the Board of Public Accountancy or the license record directly.

records.data_gap_note: The official fee-schedule page confirms the biennial/June-30/odd-even-year split exists but does not state the assignment mechanism, and an independent orchestrator re-check found no primary-source support for a license-number-parity rule. Removed rather than shipped unverified.

records.license_type_label: Firm License (CPA Corporation, Partnership, Business Corporation, LLC, or LLP)
records.cycle_description: Same biennial, June 30 schedule as individual licenses, confirmed on the same official fee page (firm fee $324 vs. $161 individual). Which specific year a given firm is due in is not resolvable from a general rule -- same gap as the individual license.

computation.note: Same gap as the individual record -- firm-specific, requires checking that firm's license record.

records.data_gap_note: Same cohort-assignment gap as the individual record; a claimed license-number-parity mechanism was checked and removed (no primary-source support) -- confirm with the Board before computing a specific firm date.

records.cycle_description: Triennial (3-year) renewal cycle. The Washington State Board of Accountancy (acb.wa.gov) states: 'All credentialed individuals must renew between January 1 and April 30 every three years.' Late-fee window is May 1-Jun 30 (+$100 late fee); the credential lapses effective July 1 if not renewed or retired by June 30. CPE must be completed by December 31 of the year prior to the credential's expiration (i.e. before the Jan 1-Apr 30 renewal window opens).

computation.note: The Board confirms the renewal WINDOW (Jan 1-Apr 30) and CYCLE LENGTH (3 years) but does not publish a birth-month, last-name, or license-number rule for which of the three years a given individual falls into -- that lives only on the individual's own license record (via WA's CPA Verify lookup tool or the Board directly). Cannot be reduced to a lookup table from state-level facts alone.

records.cycle_description: Same triennial structure as individual licenses: renewal due by April 30 every three years, $100 late fee after April 30, firm license dissolved if not renewed by June 30. The specific year within a given firm's 3-year cycle is tied to that firm's own license record, not a published statewide cohort table.

computation.note: Same gap as the individual record -- firm-specific, requires checking that firm's license record via the Board's licensee/firm search tool.

records.cycle_description: Biennial (2-year) renewal on a fixed PERSONAL anniversary date, not one common statewide date. Per the Maryland Board of Public Accountancy (dllr.state.md.us/license/cpa/cpacperep.shtml): 'Maryland CPA licenses are issued for a fixed two-year period' and 'the anniversary date of license expiration is a fixed date every two years. It cannot be changed' -- licensees are instructed to check the expiration date printed on their own license, confirming dates are individualized. 80 hours of qualifying CE (incl. 4 hours ethics) is required at the second and subsequent renewals; the first renewal after initial licensure is exempt from the CE-hour requirement. No separate CPE-reporting deadline -- CE is self-reported directly on the renewal application, due on the licensee's own expiration date.

computation.note: Next deadline = the licensee's own original license-issuance (or most recent renewal) date, advanced forward by whole multiples of 2 years. Requires the individual CPA's actual issuance/last-renewal date, which is not published at the state level -- no birth-month or license-number cohort table is used by this Board.

records.data_gap_note: A previously drafted detail claiming reminder notices are sent at 60/45/30/15/5 days before expiration was checked directly against the source page and found NOT stated there -- removed rather than shipped unverified.

records.cycle_description: Firm permits are renewed separately from individual CPA licenses (a distinct $151 firm renewal fee vs. $63 individual, per the Board's Forms & Fees page), confirming firms hold their own permit.
records.data_gap_note: No official Maryland source reviewed (Forms & Fees, Renewal, FAQs, OneStop firm-permit summary) states the firm permit's cycle length or whether it runs on a fixed calendar date, individual anniversary, or a date tied to the firm's original permit issuance. Would require direct contact with the Board (410-230-6258) or the COMAR 09.24 firm-permit regulation text to resolve.

records.cycle_description: Annual renewal, same fixed date for every licensee. The Minnesota Board of Accountancy (boa.state.mn.us) states: 'All Active and Inactive CPAs (and firms) must renew yearly by December 31 every year' and 'There is no grace period for unlicensed practice/holding out. All licenses and permits expire on December 31.' CPE operates on a separate July 1-June 30 reporting year (20 hrs/year minimum, 120 hrs/rolling 3-year period incl. 8 ethics hours); CPE for the year ending June 30 must be reported to the Board by December 31 of that same year, ahead of the license renewal.

Annual renewal, same December 31 fixed date as individual licenses, no grace period. Per the Board's own site: 100% of Minnesota-licensed owners of the firm must themselves be individually renewed by March 1 of the following year for the firm permit renewal to be valid.

Biennial. Wisconsin DSPS sets a single statutory renewal date for all individual CPA licenses: December 14 of each odd-numbered year (compliance biennium runs December 15 of an odd year through December 14 of the next odd year) -- fixed calendar, not birth-month or cohort-based; even a newly-licensed CPA still renews on this same date rather than getting an extended first cycle. CPE compliance period is the 2-year period immediately preceding renewal (80 credit hours, min 40 formal learning, 3 ethics, at least 20 hours in each 12-month half); no separate CE-reporting deadline apart from the Dec 14 renewal date.
records.data_gap_note: We could not confirm a single date against two independent authoritative sources: the Board's own page states the CPA renewal date is December 14 (citing Wis. Stat. § 440.08(2)), but the statute's own text states December 15 -- a one-day conflict between the regulator's public guidance and the law it cites. Confirm your exact renewal date with the Wisconsin DSPS Accounting Examining Board or on your license.

records.cycle_description: Same statutory date as individual licenses: December 14 of each odd-numbered year, biennial. No separate firm-specific cycle.
records.data_gap_note: We could not confirm a single date against two independent authoritative sources: the Board's own page states the firm renewal date is December 14 (citing Wis. Stat. § 440.08(2)(a)3), but the statute's own text states December 15 for accounting corporations/partnerships -- the same one-day conflict found on the individual-license record. Confirm your exact renewal date with the Wisconsin DSPS Accounting Examining Board or on your license.

records.cycle_description: Biennial, synchronized statewide (not cohort/anniversary-based). The Colorado Board of Accountancy (dpo.colorado.gov/Accountancy) states: 'All Certified Public Accountant (CPA) Certificates expire on November 30 of odd-numbered years.' Applies uniformly regardless of original issuance date, except new applicants issued within 120 days of the upcoming expiration are bumped to the following cycle's date. Renewal window opens approximately 4-5 weeks before the November 30 deadline. CPE reporting period runs January 1 of an even year through December 31 of the following odd year (80 hours total); the CPE completion due date (Dec 31 of the odd year) falls about a month after the certificate's own Nov 30 renewal date in that same year.
records.data_gap_note: We could not confirm this date against two independent authoritative sources: the Board's own page states individual CPA certificates expire November 30 of odd-numbered years, but the governing rule (3 CCR 705-1, Rule 1.9(G)) and its underlying statutes (C.R.S. 12-100-107, 12-20-202) all delegate the exact renewal schedule to an unpublished schedule set by the Division of Professions and Occupations rather than stating November 30 themselves -- so this date remains single-sourced to the Board's own page. Confirm your exact renewal date with the Colorado Board of Accountancy (DORA) or on your license.

records.cycle_description: Firm registrations run on a triennial cycle, expiring August 31 board-wide -- a fixed date shared across all firms, not each firm's own anniversary. The Colorado Board states firm registrations 'expire on August 31, every three years,' but does not publish which specific years anchor the cycle.

computation.note: Expires August 31 every 3 years for all firm registrations board-wide; next occurrence would be one of 2026-08-31, 2027-08-31, or 2028-08-31 depending on the (unconfirmed) anchor year of the cycle.

records.data_gap_note: Colorado publishes the August 31 triennial date but not the specific anchor year, so we don't show a single firm deadline. Confirm your firm's exact expiration with the Colorado Board of Accountancy (DORA) at 303-894-7800 or on your registration.

records.cycle_description: Biennial. Per A.R.S. 32-730 and A.A.C. R4-1-345(B)(1), an individual CPA certificate holder born in an even-numbered year renews during their birth month in each even-numbered year; a holder born in an odd-numbered year renews during their birth month in each odd-numbered year. Renewal must be RECEIVED by the Board (postmarks not accepted) by 5:00pm on the last business day of the birth month, or the certificate is automatically suspended ($50 late fee to reactivate; failure to renew within 3 months of suspension causes the certificate to expire). An individual/sole-proprietorship firm registration rides on this same personal cycle. CPE reporting period is defined identically to the registration period itself -- no separate CE deadline from the renewal deadline.

Biennial, but on a per-firm anniversary cycle distinct from the individual birth-month cycle. Per A.A.C. R4-1-345(B)(2), a business-organization firm (partnership, PC, PLLC, LLC, LLP) that initially registered in an even-numbered year renews during the board-approved month of its initial registration in each even-numbered year (odd-year registrants: same pattern in odd years). A sole proprietorship or individual registrant's firm registration is NOT on this separate cycle -- it renews together with the individual's personal certificate. Same 5:00pm last-business-day filing deadline and automatic-suspension consequence as the individual rule.

computation.note: Each firm's renewal month is fixed at the board-approved month set at its initial registration; the firm renews in that month during every subsequent year whose odd/even parity matches its initial-registration year. Requires that specific firm's initial-registration month and year, which is not a published state-wide fact.

records.cycle_description: Biennial, statewide-synchronized fixed calendar (not birth-month, not license-number cohort). Per Missouri Board rule 20 CSR 2010-2.070(1), licenses are issued for a two-year period beginning October 1 and expiring September 30, renewed biennially. The Board's 2026 Winter newsletter confirms the current cycle: timely renewal window for individual licenses closed September 30, 2025 (late-renewal window ran through December 31, 2025), placing the current cycle at Oct 1, 2025-Sept 30, 2027. CPE is checked on an ANNUAL calendar-year basis (40 hours/year incl. 2 ethics hours) even though the license renews only every two years; Rule 20 CSR 2010-4.010(1)(C) gives a Jan 1-Mar 1 grace period each year to cure the prior year's CPE shortfall.

Annual, statewide-synchronized fixed calendar -- a DIFFERENT cycle than individual licenses. Per Missouri Board rule 20 CSR 2010-2.072(2), firm permits are issued for a one-year period beginning November 1 and expiring October 31, renewed annually. The Board's 2026 Winter newsletter confirms: timely renewal window for firms closed October 31, 2025 (late-renewal window ran through December 31, 2025), placing the current firm-permit cycle at Nov 1, 2025-Oct 31, 2026.

records.license_type_label: Individual CPA/PA certificate and firm permit
records.cycle_description: Biennial (2-year) renewal covering both individual CPA/PA certificates and firm permits together on one shared cycle -- not two separate cycles. The Tennessee Board's guidance describes a biennial cycle with a shared expiration date and cohort (for example: a 12/31/2025 expiration, with the renewal window opening 11/2/2025 and a final late-renewal cutoff of 6/30/2026). An older administrative rule (Tenn. Comp. R. & Regs. 0020-01-.11) describes firm permits renewing annually on December 31, which conflicts with the Board's current guidance; we follow the Board's live guidance as authoritative.
records.data_gap_note: Tennessee assigns each licensee and firm to one of the two biennial years individually, so no single public date applies to everyone. Confirm your exact renewal date with the Tennessee Board of Accountancy or on your license.

records.cycle_description: CPA, PA, and AP licenses expire June 30 of every third year (e.g. 2024, 2027, 2030).
Firm permits expire June 30 of every third year (e.g. 2024, 2027, 2030) -- same triennial calendar as individual licenses, no offset.
records.ce_deadline_note: Individual CPE (120 hrs/3-yr period, min 20/yr, 12 A&A, 4 ethics) applies to the individual license renewal, not to firm permits separately.

records.cycle_description: Annual renewal. CT DCP's official CPA Renewal page states: 'CPA license and/or registration is renewed annually, expiring on December 31st of each year.' Renewal notices emailed Oct-Dec 31.
records.ce_deadline_note: CPE earned on a fiscal year (July 1-June 30, distinct from the calendar-year license), reported to the Board by Dec 31 as part of renewal (40 hrs/yr min, up to 60 reportable, 20-hr carryover from the immediately preceding fiscal year only; audit/attest hours not carryover-eligible). Source: CT DCP's separate CPE requirements page (secondary_source_url).

records.cycle_description: The CPA Firm Permit renewal form/fee is bundled on the same official page and renewal-period as the individual license (notices emailed Oct-Dec 31), which states annual/Dec 31 renewal -- but that exact sentence names only 'license and/or registration,' not 'Firm Permit' specifically, so the Dec 31 date for firm permits is inferred from bundling/context, not a directly-quoted statement.
records.data_gap_note: We could not confirm this date against two independent authoritative sources: the controlling statute (Conn. Gen. Stat. § 20-281e) explicitly delegates the firm-permit renewal date to board regulation rather than fixing December 31 itself, and the only regulation adopted under that section (RCSA §§ 20-281-1 through -12) governs quality/peer-review timing, not the expiration date -- so this date rests solely on the Board's own web pages. Confirm your exact renewal date with the CT Dept. of Consumer Protection or on your license.

records.cycle_description: Annual renewal, expires December 31 every year (Louisiana State Board of CPAs). Renewal window opens November 1; a $200 delinquent fee applies starting February (escalating $50/month through year-end).
records.ce_deadline_note: CPE completion due Dec 31; CPE reporting (submitting hours) due by Jan 31 of the following year -- a separate step from the renewal itself.

records.cycle_description: Same annual, December 31 calendar cycle as individual licenses. Renewal window opens November 1; a $300 delinquent fee applies after January 31.

Biennial, split into two fixed-date cohorts by PERMIT NUMBER PARITY -- directly quoted on the Oregon Board of Accountancy's own page: 'Licensees with an even-numbered permit expire on June 30 of even-numbered years and odd-numbered permits expire on June 30 of odd-numbered years.' Renewal due (postmarked) by June 30 to avoid a late fee; late window through August 31; lapses thereafter. The Board's page does NOT state how parity is originally assigned -- only that it determines the recurring expiration year.
records.ce_deadline_note: 80 CPE hrs/2-yr period (min 4 ethics, min 20/yr, no more than 20 hrs carryover applied to the annual minimum), deadline coincides with the June 30 renewal date.

records.cycle_description: Biennial, single fixed date for ALL firms (no cohort split): 'Public accounting firm registrations expire December 31 of odd-numbered years' per the Board's official firm renewal instructions. Postmark by Dec 31 to avoid a $265 late fee; unrenewed by Jan 31 are terminated.

Annual renewal. Per the SC Board of Accountancy (LLR)'s official FAQ: the deadline for CPA license renewal is February 1 at 11:59pm; not renewed by February 15 11:59pm is considered lapsed, requiring reinstatement. Renewal notices mailed mid-November. SCOPED TO CPA ONLY: the FAQ discusses only 'CPA' licenses -- whether the identical dates apply to PA (Public Accountant) or AP (Accounting Practitioner) categories is not stated on this page and is not asserted here.
records.ce_deadline_note: 40 CPE hrs 'for each annual reporting period' per the Board's FAQ; the page uses general phrases ('each annual reporting period,' 'each calendar year') rather than an explicit Jan 1-Dec 31 date range -- treat the exact boundary as probable but not verbatim-quoted.

records.cycle_description: Same annual, fixed February 1 (11:59pm) deadline as individual CPA licenses -- 'The deadline for firm registration renewal is February 1 at 11:59 pm. If a firm registration is not renewed by February 15 at 11:59 pm, it is considered lapsed.'

records.license_type_label: Individual CPA/PA/non-licensee owner and firm permit
records.cycle_description: Fixed annual cycle, same for every individual AND firm licensee (not birth-month, not cohort). Per Ala. Admin. Code 30-X-5-.05(1): annual permits to practice 'expire on the last day of September of each year.' Per Ala. Admin. Code 30-X-3-.07(1): the renewal fee ($100 individual / $130 firm) and CPE compliance statement must be submitted 'no later than December 31 following expiration of the permit.' Late penalties (30-X-3-.07(4)): $100 if filed Jan1-Feb, $500 if filed March, discipline possible after.
records.ce_deadline_note: CPE (40 hrs, incl. 8 A&A + 2 ethics) must be completed within the fiscal year ending Sept 30 (30-X-5-.02(1)) -- earlier than the Dec 31 deadline for submitting the renewal + signed CPE compliance statement (30-X-3-.07(1)).

records.cycle_description: Biennial. Online renewal window July 1-31. Cohort by LICENSE NUMBER PARITY, directly quoted on the Board's own page: 'CPAs with odd-numbered licenses renew their license in odd-numbered years. Those with even-numbered licenses renew their license in even-numbered years.' Late window (higher fee) through Sept 1; lapses after.
records.ce_deadline_note: CPE reporting period is the two calendar years immediately preceding the renewal year, distinct from the July 31 deadline itself.

records.cycle_description: Biennial, single fixed schedule for ALL firms (not license-number split): renew online in July of EVEN-numbered years. Standard fee through Aug 1; late fee through Sept 1; expires and requires Board reinstatement thereafter.
records.data_gap_note: We could not confirm the year-parity for this date against two independent authoritative sources: the Board's own page states firm permits renew biennially, due August 1 of even-numbered years, and both the implementing regulation (201 KAR 1:081) and a separate statute (KRS 325.301(5)) independently confirm the biennial cycle and the August 1 deadline -- but no source states 'even-numbered years' specifically, so that detail remains single-sourced to the Board's own page. Confirm your exact renewal date with the Kentucky State Board of Accountancy or on your license.

records.license_type_label: Individual CPA Certificate / Permit to Practice
records.cycle_description: Annual renewal, genuine birth-month personalized cycle. Okla. Stat. tit. 59 Section 15.14A: 'All permits issued shall be renewed on the last day of the individual's birth month.' The Board's own site publishes a birth-month lookup table with grace period + auto-revocation dates.

computation.rule: Expires last day of the licensee's birth month, every year -- same shape as Texas.

records.ce_deadline_note: CPE tracked on a calendar-year basis, a separate cycle from the birth-month renewal date; the Board's table pairs each birth-month deadline with a specific CPE Reporting Year that typically lags the renewal date.

records.license_type_label: Firm Registration / Firm Permit to Practice
records.cycle_description: Annual, fixed calendar deadline separate from individuals' birth-month cycle. The Board's own Firm Registration Renewal Form states in bold: 'FORM & FEE MUST BE RECEIVED BY JUNE 30, [year].'

records.license_type_label: Iowa CPA/LPA Individual Certificate or License
records.cycle_description: Annual renewal -- every CPA certificate and LPA license (Iowa Code 542.6/542.8/542.19) expires June 30 each year, same date for every licensee. Iowa Admin. Code 193A-5.3(1): 'Licenses expire on June 30 of each year.' A renewal notice typically goes out in the May preceding expiration; a 30-day grace period follows a missed deadline before the license lapses (193A-5.4).
records.ce_deadline_note: 120 CPE hrs (min 4 ethics) over a rolling 3-year period ending Dec 31 or June 30 immediately preceding the July 1 renewal (licensee self-selects which end-date applies).

records.cycle_description: Iowa Code 542.7 establishes a firm permit to practice, separate from individual certificates/licenses. The administrative rule chapter governing individual license renewal (Iowa Admin. Code 193A-Chapter 5) explicitly excludes firm permits from its scope, so whether firms share the individual June 30 date or run on a separate cycle is not confirmed.
records.data_gap_note: We have not located the specific rule governing Iowa firm-permit renewal dates, so we do not show one here. Confirm your firm's exact renewal date with the Iowa Accountancy Examining Board or on your permit.

records.license_type_label: Individual CPA license and firm permit
records.cycle_description: Biennial. Both the individual CPA license and the CPA firm permit expire December 31 of every even-numbered year -- a single fixed date for the entire licensee/firm population, no cohort split.

records.license_type_label: Individual CPA license and firm permit
records.cycle_description: Biennial. Both individual CPA licenses and CPA firm permits expire December 31 of every even-numbered year -- confirmed by the statewide DOPL renewal-dates rule listing the same date for both license types, no cohort split.

Biennial. A holder's permit to practice expires on the next July 1 that matches the parity of their certificate number: odd-numbered certificates expire in odd-numbered years, even-numbered certificates expire in even-numbered years (K.S.A. 1-310(b),(c)).
records.data_gap_note: Your exact renewal date depends on whether your Kansas CPA certificate number is odd or even -- we cannot determine that from state-level data alone. Confirm your renewal date with the Kansas Board of Accountancy or on your certificate.

records.cycle_description: Annual. Every CPA firm registration must be renewed by December 31 of each year -- a single fixed date for all firms, no cohort split.

Annual. The permit to practice expires December 31 of each year (NRS 628.380(2)); renewal fees may be paid without a late penalty through January 31 of the following year, but the permit itself expires December 31.

Nevada CPA-firm offices must register annually with the Board, but we could not confirm a specific 'registration expires on X date' provision distinct from the annual report-filing deadline (January 31).
records.data_gap_note: We could not confirm a firm-specific expiration date against two independent authoritative sources: the statute (NRS 628.370) sets only an annual registration cadence with no calendar date, and the administrative-code citation we found (NAC 628.180) governs the annual REPORT filing deadline (January 31), not a registration expiration date -- these are not necessarily the same thing. Confirm your exact renewal date with the Nevada Board of Accountancy or on your registration.

records.cycle_description: Annual. All Arkansas individual CPA/PA licenses expire December 31 of each year and renew annually for a one-year term.
Annual. All Arkansas firm registrations expire December 31 of each year and renew annually for a one-year term (Ark. Code Ann. § 17-12-504(b)).

Biennial. Individual permits expire June 30 of the first year after issuance in which the holder's age becomes divisible by two -- in practice this splits individuals into even-birth-year and odd-birth-year cohorts (Neb. Rev. Stat. § 1-136(1)(a),(2)(b)).
records.data_gap_note: Your exact renewal date depends on your birth-year parity -- we cannot determine that from state-level data alone. Confirm your renewal date with the Nebraska Board of Public Accountancy or on your permit.

records.cycle_description: Annual. Unlike individual permits (which are biennial and birth-year-cohort split), firm permits expire June 30 of every year for every firm -- confirmed by the Board's own live renewal-notice page distinguishing 'all CPA firms' from the individual cohort split (Neb. Rev. Stat. § 1-136(2)(a)).

Annual. Each individual license has a staggered expiration date tied to the licensee's own birth month -- renewal (fee + CPE report) is due by the last day of that birth month every year (16.60.3.9.I NMAC).
records.data_gap_note: Your exact renewal date depends on your birth month -- we cannot determine that from state-level data alone. Confirm your renewal date with the New Mexico Regulation and Licensing Department or on your license.

records.cycle_description: Firm permits are issued/renewed for terms of up to two years, always expiring June 30, but which calendar year applies to a given firm depends on when that firm's permit was last issued or renewed -- printed on the firm's own permit, not published per-firm by the board (Section 61-28B-13.E NMSA 1978).
records.data_gap_note: Firm permits recur on a June 30 anchor date, but the specific year (a firm may be on a 1- or 2-year term) is printed on your firm's own permit, not available from state-level data. Confirm your exact renewal date with the New Mexico Regulation and Licensing Department or on your firm permit.

records.cycle_description: Annual. Every individual Mississippi CPA license holder must register and pay the annual registration fee on or before January 1 each year (Miss. Code § 73-33-7(2)).
Annual. Every CPA firm holding a Mississippi permit must register on or before January 1 each year, the identical statutory deadline as individual licenses (Miss. Code § 73-33-7(3)).

Idaho is mid-transition (announced May 2026) from annual to biennial renewal, staggered by birth-year parity. The current codified rule still states annual June 30 renewal, while the Board's own site describes an in-progress biennial rollout -- a genuine conflict between two current official sources during the transition.
records.data_gap_note: Idaho is in the middle of a licensure-cycle change (announced May 2026): the Board's site describes a new birth-year-parity biennial schedule, but the current administrative rule (IDAPA 24.30.01 § 108) still states annual June 30 renewal with no cohort/biennial language yet. We can't safely compute your next date during this transition. Confirm your exact renewal date with the Idaho Division of Occupational and Professional Licenses or on your license.

records.cycle_description: Annual, unaffected by the individual-license transition described above. Firm registration must be renewed no later than September 30 each year (IDAPA 24.30.01 § 211.01).
Annual. The CPA certificate runs on a July 1 - June 30 cycle and expires each year on June 30 (W. Va. Code § 30-9-12(a)).

Firm permits appear to follow the same annual July 1 - June 30 cycle as individual certificates (W. Va. Code § 30-9-17(h)). We're confirming the exact firm renewal date and will publish it once verified.
records.data_gap_note: West Virginia firm permits appear to follow the same annual June 30 cycle as individual certificates (W. Va. Code § 30-9-17(h)); we're confirming the exact firm date before publishing it. In the meantime, confirm your renewal date with the West Virginia Board of Accountancy or on your permit.

records.cycle_description: Annual. All Montana license categories (CPA, Licensed Public Accountant) and firm registration expire December 31 of each year -- a single fixed date, no cohort split.

records.license_type_label: Individual CPA/PA permit and firm permit
records.cycle_description: Biennial. All Delaware Board of Accountancy permits -- individual CPA, individual PA, and firm permits -- expire June 30 of odd-numbered years (24 Del. C. § 108(b): permits 'shall expire on June 30 of the odd-numbered year following issuance or renewal'; § 111(c) confirms firm permits are 'renewed biennially').
Annual. Every CPA/LPA certificate is due for renewal June 30 each year (late-fee window through July 31, involuntary relinquishment effective August 1) -- N.D. Admin. Code § 3-02-02-04.
Annual. Every firm permit is due for renewal June 30 each year, the same calendar as individual certificates (late-fee window through July 31) -- N.D. Admin. Code § 3-02-02-04.1.

Biennial. All individual CPA/public-accountant licenses share one statewide fixed licensing period -- currently January 1, 2026 to December 31, 2027 -- with no cohort/parity/birth-month split (AS 08.04.105(a)).
Biennial. All CPA firm permits share the same statewide fixed licensing period as individual licenses -- currently January 1, 2026 to December 31, 2027 (12 AAC 04.280).

Biennial. Individual CPA licenses renew every two years on July 31 of odd-numbered years -- 26 V.S.A. § 75(a) sets the 2-year cycle, Code of Vermont Rules 04-030-010 Rule 8.1(b) fixes the specific date.

The statute (26 V.S.A. § 75(a)) sets a 2-year renewal cycle for firm registrations bundled with individual licenses, but we could not confirm firm registrations share the individual license's specific July 31 date -- the administrative rule's date-setting clause uses the narrower word 'Licenses' only, not 'firm registrations,' where the same rule elsewhere explicitly says both when it means both.
records.data_gap_note: The 2-year renewal cycle for firm registrations is confirmed by statute, but we could not confirm firm registrations share individual licenses' specific July 31 date against two independent sources -- the codified rule that fixes that date refers only to 'Licenses,' not firm registrations, in the exact clause that sets the day. Confirm your exact renewal date with the Vermont Office of Professional Regulation or on your registration.

records.cycle_description: Annual. All active and inactive CPA certificates expire December 31 each year (W.S. 33-3-109(d)).
Annual. All CPA firm permits expire December 31 of each year, the same calendar as individual certificates (W.S. 33-3-120(b)).

records.license_type_label: Individual CPA license and firm license
records.cycle_description: The Board's own page states both individual CPA licenses and firm licenses renew annually on September 30, and the codified rule independently confirms all licenses expire annually with no cohort split -- but the codified rule never states the specific September 30 date itself.
records.data_gap_note: We could not confirm the specific September 30 date against two independent authoritative sources: the codified rule (Maine Board of Accountancy Rule, Ch. 5 & 6) confirms the annual cycle but never states a specific day of the year, leaving the exact date single-sourced to the Board's own page. Confirm your exact renewal date with the Maine Board of Accountancy or on your license.

records.license_type_label: Individual CPA/PA certificate and firm permit
records.cycle_description: The Board's own newsletter (archived) states individual and firm renewals are due around August 1 each year, but we could not independently re-verify the current administrative rule -- the state's live board site (dlr.sd.gov) returned repeated server errors during our research, and a third-party mirror of the relevant rule section was found to be stale (the rule was amended in 2025).
records.data_gap_note: We could not confirm this date against two independent, currently-valid authoritative sources: the South Dakota Board of Accountancy's own site was unreachable (server errors) during our research, and the administrative rule text we found on a third-party legal mirror turned out to be outdated (the rule was amended in 2025 and no longer covers renewal in the same way). Confirm your exact renewal date with the South Dakota Department of Labor and Regulation, Accountancy Program, or on your license.

records.license_type_label: Individual CPA/PA permit and firm permit
records.cycle_description: Triennial. Every permit (individual and firm) is valid for 3 years and expires on June 30 of its scheduled year (R.I. Gen. Laws §§ 5-3.1-7, 5-3.1-9). Rhode Island staggers its licensee population across three parallel cohorts (~1/3 due each year), so which year a given permit is next due depends on when it was last issued or renewed.
records.data_gap_note: Your exact renewal date depends on which of Rhode Island's three rotating 3-year cohorts your permit is in -- we cannot determine that from state-level data alone. Confirm your renewal date with the Rhode Island Board of Accountancy or on your permit.

records.license_type_label: Individual CPA license/permit and firm permit
records.cycle_description: Biennial. The CPA license (HRS § 466-5(j)) and the individual and firm permits to practice (HRS § 466-7) all expire December 31 of every odd-numbered year -- the same fixed date across all three credential types, no cohort split.

records.license_type_label: Individual CPA certificate/PA registration and firm permit
records.cycle_description: Biennial, but not a fixed calendar date for everyone: each license (CPA certificate, PA registration, or firm permit) expires exactly 2 years from that specific license's own issuance or last-renewal date (RSA 310:8, II; RSA 309-B:7, II), following a 2024 legislative rewrite that replaced New Hampshire's older fixed-calendar scheme.
records.data_gap_note: Your exact renewal date is exactly 2 years from when your license/permit was last issued or renewed -- a personal fact we can't determine from state-level data. Confirm your renewal date with the NH Office of Professional Licensure and Certification or on your license.
